titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Early Yale Documents Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michael Lotstein, Emily Komornik, and Alison Clemens

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 29, 2020 at 07:00

did.unittitle: Early Yale documents collection
did.abstract: Correspondence, minutes, financial records, charters and official acts, inventories of books and other property, and other papers documenting the founding and early history of Yale College. Documents include the original charter of the Collegiate School; letters by and to the first trustees of the school; and the 1718 inventory of books donated to the school by Elihu Yale.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, 2018. Acquired by gift and purchase from various sources.

userestrict.head: Ownership & Copyright
userestrict.p: The Early Yale Documents Collection is the physical property of Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Early Yale Documents Collection (RU 1154). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: This finding aid was produced from a previously existing card set in the Beinecke Library Manuscripts Catalog, or from another inventory. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained.
Former call numbers: GEN MS VAULT SECT. 17; GEN MSS 856.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: The Early Yale Documents Collection consists of correspondence, minutes, financial records, charters and official acts, inventories, and other papers documenting the founding and early history of Yale College from its early eighteenth century origins as the Collegiate School until the mid-eighteenth century, as well as some miscellaneous Yale-related material from the late eighteenth and nineteenth centuries. The collection spans the dates 1701-1889, with the bulk of the material dating from between 1701 and 1751.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 45 (YRG 45), Yale objects and memorabilia.

did.unittitle: Documents concerning Yale's founding
"An Act for Liberty to Erect a Collegiate School" Yale Charter
Original case for the Yale Charter
"Proposals for Erecting an University, in the Renowned Colony of Connecticut"
Letter advising about a college in Connecticut; Boston
Letter to James Pierpont, on a plan for a College; Boston
Letter to the Connecticut Ministers planning a College; Wethersfield
Letter to A. Pierson; New Haven
Letter to Abraham Pierson; Windsor
Letter to Thomas Buckingham; Boston
Offer of land and materials for a college building; New Haven
Official manuscript copy in an unidentified hand
Letter to Thomas Buckingham, Abraham Pierson, James Pierpont; Windsor
Letter to Reverend James Pierpont, with excuses for not attending a college meeting; Stonington
Letter to James Pierpont, Boston
Autograph copy, signed, of the resolution passed by the Trustees at a meeting held in Say-brook
Letter from the Trustees, in the handwriting of James Pierpont, to Nathaniel Lynde; Saybrook
Letters to John Winthrop, James [Fitche?], and Reverend Stallonstall
Document authorizing the Trustees to raise money for the Collegiate School
Statement to Students, Branford
Letter to James Pierpont
Circular letter to College Trustees; Killingworth
Draft of Resolutions for the Trustees
Letter to Josiah Willard Gibbs; Burlington, Massachusetts
Copy of a letter made in 1789, under the direction of President Stiles
Extract made by E.C. Herrick from a letter from Dummer to James Pierpont; Whitehall
Proceedings of the Trustees
Subscriptions for the settlement of the school in New Haven
Proceedings of the Trustees for September 12, 1716, signed by Moses Noyes and Joseph Webb, at Saybrook
Authorization by the College Trustees for building in New Haven
A copy of ye Remonstrance against the settling of the Collegiate School at New Haven
Memorial of a minority of the Trustees
Call for a meeting of the Trustees
Letter to Samuel Johnson
Memoranda by Daniel Buckingham in behalf of Saybrook
Opinion by Daniel Buckingham on the removal of the school from Saybrook
Orders of the assembly for hearing the Trustees
Message of the Upper House to the Trustees
Draft of a memorial to Connecticut General Assembly, New Haven
Observations and remarks by Timothy Woodbridge and Thomas Buckingham
Resolves of the Upper House
Message from the Upper House
Order of the assembly for a hearing of the Trustees
Answer of the Trustees to the observations and remarks of Woodbridge and Buckingham
Account by the college trustees of the removal to New Haven
A reply to the reason for the votes of the Lower House
Votes of the assembly on requiring unanimity of the Trustees
Proceedings of the assembly on confirmation of the removal to New Haven
Proposal of the Upper House for a conference
Proceedings of the assembly on the determination of a place for the school
Resolves of the General Assembly at New Haven, October 10, 1717. Signed by Hez Wyllys. Contemporary copy
Letter from the Trustees to Jeremiah Dummer, in the hand of John Davenport, New Haven
Historical remarks concerning the collegiate school, New Haven
Contemporary manuscript copy of a letter to the Trustees of the collegiate school
"A List of Books Given to Yale College" by Jeremiah Dummer

odd.head: General
odd.p: Item not received when collection was transferred from the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

did.unittitle: Draft of a letter from the Trustees to T. Woodbridge and T. Buckingham
Three letters, probably in the hand of tutor Samuel Johnson, with autographed signatures of the trustees
Draft of a letter of Trustees to Cotton Mather
Invitation for the return of seceded students, New Haven
Contemporary manuscript copy of an act for the encouragement of Yale College
Letter to Samuel Russel, New London
Brief issued by the assembly
"An accompt of sale of sundry goods and merchandizes…"
Letter to the Trustees of Yale College, New Haven
Action of the assembly respecting a seal
Letter to the Trustees of Yale College, declining a call to the Rectorship, Boston
"An Act in Explanation of and Addition to the Act for Erecting a Collegiate School in this Colony…"
Action of the assembly on the appointment of a Rector
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly
Petition of the Trustees to the assembly
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly
Memorial of Elisha Williams to the assembly
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly, with action thereon
Proceedings of the Trustees, in regard to the gifts of Bishop Berkeley
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly
Proceedings of the Trustees, December 20, 1732, in regard to the gifts of Bishop Berkeley to Yale University
Incomplete proceedings of the Trustees, in regard to the gifts of Bishop Berkeley to Yale University
Letter to Rector Williams
Proceedings of the Trustees in regards to the gifts of Bishop Berkeley
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly
Account of the income and expenses of the college
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly
Proceedings of the Trustees, September 11, 1734, in regard to the gifts of Bishop Berkeley to Yale University
Incomplete proceedings of the Trustees in regard to the appointment of Thomas Clap
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly
Report of a committee of the assembly on the needs of the college
Memorial of the Trustees to the assembly
Proceedings of the Trustees, September 9, 1741, regarding the discipline of students
Report of the committee of the assembly, relating to the college
Memorial of Daniel Edwards and Samuel Mix to the assembly, concerning repairs at the college
Photocopy
Documents concerning the early history of Yale
Propositions for the improvement of Yale College in the colony of Connecticut

odd.head: General
odd.p: Document not received from the Beinecke Library. Folder 40 not used.

did.unittitle: Request of seven Connecticut ministers to their brethren, recommending adoption of the Westminster Confession
Letter July 14, 1888 to Franklin B. Dexter
A list of books given to the college of Connecticut in New England with the names of the benefactors
Letter to Joseph Noyes about shipment of books for the college; Boston
List of books, "the gift of Senior John Davie to the Colledge of Connecticott, except one fo no 17…which was given by one Mr. William Rowe
A catalogue of the books sent by ye honorable Elihu Yale, esquire
"A catalogue of books given from Great Britain to the Collegiate School of Connecticut, at New Haven…"
Manuscript copy of his salutatory oration
An account of some books received from Great Britain from Dr. Turner
Letter to court in New London on suit against Yale College, Canterbury
A catalogue of books for Yale College, sent by Bishop Berkeley. Invoice at the end signed "Henry Newman"
A catalogue of books, chiefly mathematical, to be sent to "Yale College"
Manuscript notebook, probably kept while he was the congregational minister at East Guilford, now Madison
Latin dissertation, Yale College
"This book given to the Library of Yale College, by the Reverend Mr. Henry Barclay, N, 1750, New York"
Latin dissertation, written while in Yale College
Letter to John Noyes; Hatfield, Massachusetts
Oration, in his autograph, delivered by him at Yale College
Letter to his son, Josiah Willard, a student at Yale College; Newington
Letter to his son, Josiah Willard, a student at Yale College; Newington
Letters to Josiah Willard, Yale College
Letter to Denys Debert, suggesting that he aid Dr. William S. Johnson in securing help for Yale College
Letter to Lucretia Taylor (afterwards Mrs. Samuel Nott) containing a map of Yale College; New Haven
A series of twenty-five letters sent to his father, Thomas, to himself while in Yale, as well as other Yalemen
Ecomium on literature; autograph manuscript of S. Baldwin; New Haven
Two manuscript accounts of Yale undergraduates
"Some strictures of the life and character of Doctor Lothrop deceased"; manuscript in the hand of Benjamin Lord
Manuscript-composition-book in an unidentified hand, dated "Y. College August 30th, 1782"
Letter to the Reverend Ezra Stiles acknowledging receipt of a copy of Stile's election sermon
Miscellany: Verietate Natura Gaudet
Laws of Yale College, manuscript, in an unidentified hand or hands, beginning "Index, the charter of Yale College"
Letter to Charles Whittlesey; Haddam
Circular letter in an unidentified autograph
Expenditures during a college course, 1835-1839, manuscript in the hand of George Sherman; New Haven
General items relating to H.M. Boot while a student at Yale College
Letter to his father; Huntington Poter, New Haven
Committee for memorial to Charles H. Porter
Extracts from an original manuscript book of records of the governor and council of the colony of Connecticut
Notebook with their constitution
Two copies of an autograph letter in an unidentified hand, signed "a sophomore"
Two manuscript notebooks, in an unidentified hand
Autograph biographical notes, in an unidentified handwriting, on members of the class of 1856, Yale College
A list of names, signed with the individual autographs
Papers, both manuscript and printed
Manuscript catalogue of books his father, the Reverend Abraham Pierson, intended to give to Yale College
Scrapbook containing newspaper and magazine clippings related to Yale University
"An act for the more full and compleat establishment of Yale College in New-Haven and for enlarging the powers and previleges thereof."
